Politics.headline: Tamil Nadu government formation LIVE: TN Assembly to convene first session; Speaker election on May 12
Politics.detail: C. Joseph Vijay was sworn in as Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu, becoming the first member of a minority religious denomination (Christian) to occupy the top post in the State since 1967; he signed papers allowing 200 units of free electricity for domestic consumers, for setting up a special force for women's safety and also for anti-drug trafficking units across the State. Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated C. Joseph Vijay on taking oath as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister.

Politics.headline: Pope Leo's American roots give him unique political power
Politics.detail: Pope Leo's American background is giving him unique political power on the world stage. Tensions are emerging in the Netanyahu-Trump alliance, with reports that Washington no longer consults him.

Politics.headline: Iran threatens US sites in Middle East if tankers come under fire
Politics.detail: Amid ongoing tensions, Iran has issued a threat to target U.S. sites in the Middle East if Iranian oil tankers are attacked. The U.S. awaits Iran's response to a proposed peace deal.

Politics.headline: Peter Magyar sworn in as Hungary's PM, ending Orban's 16 years in power
Politics.detail: Peter Magyar has been sworn in as the new Prime Minister of Hungary, bringing an end to Viktor Orban's 16-year tenure. This marks a significant shift in Hungarian politics.

Politics.headline: Republicans who denied 2020 election results could be governors next year
Politics.detail: Republicans who have denied the 2020 election results are positioned to potentially become governors in the upcoming year. With GOP wins on election maps, Democrats have a steeper climb to victory.

Technology.headline: Tesla's Latest Recall Could Mean Wheels Flying Off the Cybertruck
Technology.detail: The Tesla Cybertruck's wheel studs could eventually separate from the wheel hub on some affected vehicles, according to a report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), potentially causing the wheel to detach after continued use and strain, increasing the risk of a collision.

Technology.headline: Vibe Coding Is Causing 'Thousands' of Data Security Vulnerabilities
Intel Reportedly Planning 4 New CPU Lines Through 2028: Here's What to Expect
Hey, PlayStation Gamers. Sony May Owe You a Cut of Its $7.85 Million Settlement
Meta sues Ofcom over fines regime for breaches of Online Safety Act
Technology.detail: Meta is challenging Ofcom's fines regime related to breaches of the Online Safety Act.

Science.headline: Scientists successfully transfer longevity gene and extend lifespan
Science.detail: Scientists at the University of Rochester transferred a longevity-related gene from naked mole rats into mice, resulting in healthier and longer-lived mice, demonstrating that longevity traits can be adapted beyond the species in which they evolved. The special gene boosts the production of high molecular weight hyaluronic acid, which appears to protect against cancer, reduce inflammation and support healthier aging.

Science.headline: Antarctica is melting from below and scientists say it's worse than expected
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered that long channels beneath Antarctic ice shelves trap warmer ocean water, leading to faster-than-expected melting from below and potentially accelerating global sea-level rise. Even regions of East Antarctica, once considered stable, may be more vulnerable, and current climate models may underestimate future sea-level rise due to this process.

Science.headline: Scientists say a critical Atlantic ocean current is weakening and the world could feel the impact
Science.detail: A major Atlantic Ocean current system, which regulates global climate, has been weakening for nearly two decades, potentially affecting storms, rainfall, sea levels, and winter conditions in Europe and North America. The slowdown, detected across a vast region of the North Atlantic, provides strong evidence that the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC) is losing strength.

Science.headline: “Cannot be explained” – New ultra stainless steel stuns researchers
Science.detail: A team at the University of Hong Kong has created a new "super steel" that can withstand the harsh conditions required for green hydrogen production from seawater, using an unexpected double-protection mechanism that resists corrosion better than conventional stainless steel. This new material could replace the expensive titanium parts currently used in hydrogen systems.

Science.headline: Scientists discover the brain's hidden “stop scratching” switch
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered a hidden "stop-scratching" signal in the nervous system, which tells the brain when enough scratching has occurred, centering on a molecule called TRPV4 that acts as an internal braking system for itch relief. Mice lacking this signal scratched less often but couldn't stop when they did, suggesting that this mechanism naturally limits scratching.

Health.headline: My egg, my wife's womb, our baby: how we found our way to lesbian motherhood
Health.detail: A personal account of a lesbian couple's journey to motherhood through reciprocal IVF.

Health.headline: Scientists Say This Simple Music Trick Can Boost Workout Endurance by 20%
Health.detail: A new study shows that listening to your own favorite workout music can dramatically boost endurance, with cyclists lasting nearly 20% longer when riding with self-selected songs compared to silence.

Health.headline: UK passengers on hantavirus-hit cruise to be flown to Merseyside for quarantine
Health.detail: UK passengers who were on the cruise ship affected by the hantavirus outbreak will be flown to Merseyside for quarantine.

Health.headline: Argentina in spotlight over hantavirus as authorities retrace footsteps of ship's passengers.
Health.detail: Authorities in Argentina are retracing the steps of passengers from the hantavirus-affected cruise ship to monitor and contain the spread.

Health.headline: 'Empty and vapid' CDC finally responds to hantavirus outbreak. But experts say it's too little, too late
Health.detail: Experts are critical of the CDC's response to the hantavirus outbreak, deeming it insufficient and delayed, while the WHO has been leading the response.

Sports.headline: Hurricanes oust Flyers; Avs suffer first loss
Sports.detail: Carolina's Jackson Blake scored in overtime to eliminate the Flyers in Game 4 of their Stanley Cup Playoff series, while the Avalanche suffered their first loss of the playoffs.

Sports.headline: Ajay Mitchell is the Thunder's latest emergent star after his best career playoff game vs Lakers
Sports.detail: Ajay Mitchell had a career playoff high with 24 points and 10 assists in the Thunder's 131-108 victory over the Lakers, taking a 3-0 lead in the series. He has stepped up in place of the injured Jalen Williams.

Sports.headline: Leipzig secure Champions League place as Stuttgart boost top-four hopes
Sports.detail: RB Leipzig secured a Champions League spot with a 2-1 victory over St. Pauli, while Stuttgart improved their chances of finishing in the top four with a 3-1 win against Bayer Leverkusen. Hoffenheim remains level on points with Stuttgart after defeating Werder Bremen.

Sports.headline: Brewers rally in 10th to win 4-3 as Yankees waste brilliant performance from Cam Schlittler
Sports.detail: William Contreras hit a sacrifice fly in the 10th inning as the Brewers rallied to beat the Yankees 4-3, after Contreras also singled home the tying run in the eighth inning. Schlittler pitched six scoreless innings.

Sports.headline: NRL 2026: Canberra Raiders vs Penrith Panthers, match wrap, final scores, videos, highlights, Nathan Cleary
Sports.detail: The Penrith Panthers defeated the Canberra Raiders 30-18, consolidating their position at the top of the ladder. Lindsay Smith and Ethan Strange impressed, while Simi Sasagi sustained an injury.

Entertainment.headline: Britney Spears Says She's on 'Spiritual Journey,' Posts Photo of a Snake
Entertainment.detail: Britney Spears shares that she's on a 'spiritual journey' and posts a photo of a snake.

Entertainment.headline: Double Murderer Betty Broderick Dead
Entertainment.detail: Betty Broderick, who was convicted of double murder, has died.

Entertainment.headline: Japan's Haruki Murakami to publish first new novel in 3 years with 'The Tale of KAHO' in July
Entertainment.detail: Haruki Murakami will release his first novel in three years, titled 'The Tale of KAHO,' in July.

Entertainment.headline: Savannah Guthrie Leaves 'Today' Show During Live Broadcast
Entertainment.detail: Savannah Guthrie had to leave the 'Today' show during a live broadcast.

Entertainment.headline: K. Michelle on Why RHOA Was the Way to Reenter Reality TV, 10 Years After Love & Hip Hop (Exclusive)
Entertainment.detail: K. Michelle discusses her decision to rejoin reality TV with RHOA after a decade since her appearance on Love & Hip Hop.

Crime & Justice.headline: Appeal to locate woman missing from Morisset
Crime & Justice.detail: NSW Police are appealing for public assistance to locate a woman missing from Morisset, Australia.

Crime & Justice.headline: Appeal to locate teenage girl missing from Raymond Terrace
Crime & Justice.detail: Police are seeking assistance to find a teenage girl who has gone missing from Raymond Terrace, Australia.

Crime & Justice.headline: Woman charged following alleged offensive language incident - Maroubra
Crime & Justice.detail: A woman has been charged by NSW Police following an incident involving offensive language in Maroubra, Australia.

Crime & Justice.headline: Kidnapping of El Chapo's cartel partner hangs over U.S. indictment of Mexican governor
Crime & Justice.detail: The U.S. indictment against a Mexican governor is linked to the kidnapping of Sinaloa cartel leader Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada, potentially triggering political and criminal consequences on both sides of the border.

Crime & Justice.headline: Colorado's Legion of Doom
Crime & Justice.detail: An article discusses Colorado state senators and their legislative actions concerning sentencing for violent crimes, including murder, and their potential impact on public safety and recidivism.

Education.headline: CBSE Class 12 Result 2026 "Coming Soon" On DigiLocker; Will Marksheets Be Released Today?
Education.detail: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) Class 12 results for 2026 are expected soon and will be available on DigiLocker, with speculation about marksheets being released today.

Education.headline: NTA Says NEET-UG 2026 Conducted Under Strict Security, Cooperating With Probe Into Alleged Irregularities
Education.detail: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has stated that the NEET-UG 2026 exam was conducted with strict security measures and that they are cooperating with investigations into alleged irregularities.

Education.headline: Haryana Board Result 2026 When? Check Class 10, 12 Previous Years' Dates
Education.detail: Students are awaiting the Haryana Board results for classes 10 and 12, and can check previous years' dates to get an idea of when the results might be released.

Education.headline: Parents who want Irish-language education for their children 'are being failed by State'
Education.detail: Parents seeking Irish-language education for their children are reportedly being let down by the government.

Education.headline: UAE schools and universities to resume in-person classes from Monday
Education.detail: The Ministry of Education has announced that all public and private schools, universities, and nurseries across the UAE will resume in-person learning on Monday, May 11, 2026, after a period of remote learning. Safety protocols will continue to be implemented.

Lifestyle.headline: In HelloNation, Real Estate Expert Howard Goldberg Explores the Fort Lauderdale Lifestyle
Lifestyle.detail: The article examines how lifestyle habits, traffic patterns, and neighborhood design influence homebuying decisions in Fort Lauderdale, highlighting the importance of walkability, commute times, and seasonal rhythms.

Lifestyle.headline: Emotional burden of infertility can undermine lifestyle choices that may help women conceive
Lifestyle.detail: A global conference on assisted reproduction in Beijing heard that the emotional burden of infertility actively undermines the capacity of many women to make sustained lifestyle changes, including weight loss and physical activity, that can improve their chances of becoming pregnant. Up to 60% of affected women experience mental health challenges.

Lifestyle.headline: She made it look like love, not sacrifice
Lifestyle.detail: A daughter reflects on her mother's unwavering love and support, recognizing the sacrifices made without ever making them feel like a burden, and appreciating the strength and grace with which she navigated life.

Lifestyle.headline: Why do luxury hotels smell so good?
Lifestyle.detail: Fragrances are not decoration, but rather an integral part of a hotel's infrastructure, as a well-designed scent has a significant impact on a guest's memory.

Lifestyle.headline: 'Wakaf' spirit lives on as Narathiwat museum showcases 1,000-year-old Malaysian-linked Quran manuscripts
Lifestyle.detail: The Narathiwat museum is showcasing 1,000-year-old Malaysian-linked Quran manuscripts, highlighting the enduring 'Wakaf' spirit.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Politicians pump up their pay on the public's tab
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Colorado legislators gave themselves an automatic pay raise while the state faces a $1.5 billion budget deficit, drawing criticism for being out of touch with everyday citizens. The pay hikes, effective with the coming New Year, will increase House members' salaries by 2.8% to $48,893 per year and Senate members' salaries by 4.5% to about $49,701.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: How the Supreme Court tariffs case wasn't won
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This Washington Post opinion piece discusses the legal battle over President Donald Trump's tariff powers, highlighting a lawyer's self-promotion and the strategic roles of different legal minds in the case. The author argues that the challengers won because the law was on their side, not because of the oral argument delivered by a particular lawyer.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Israel's national security should not be sold out by its citizens - editorial
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Jerusalem Post emphasizes that no crisis justifies endangering the country for payment, referencing a recent indictment of a civilian and three soldiers who allegedly maintained contact with Iranian intelligence. The editorial stresses the need for prevention through public messaging, enforcement, education, and strengthening civic life to foster a sense of belonging.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Beijing is afraid of a strong Japan
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This Taipei Times editorial argues that Beijing is wary of a strong Japan, highlighting Japan's updated "Free and Open Indo-Pacific" strategy and its defensive missile installations on Yonaguni Island. It contrasts Southeast Asian countries' confidence in Japan with concerns over China's assertiveness in regional disputes.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Tokyo should heed the call of the times for peace and cooperation: China Daily editorial
Opinion/Editorial.detail: China Daily criticizes Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's leadership for charting a course that threatens regional stability through constitutional revision, military expansion, and strategic alliances. The editorial expresses concern over Japan's departure from its post-World War II commitment to peace and the revival of militaristic tendencies.

World.headline: Passengers begin evacuating cruise ship hit by hantavirus
World.detail: A Dutch-flagged cruise ship that was hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak reached Spain's Canary Islands early Sunday morning, and passengers are beginning to disembark. Several countries have been affected by the outbreak.

World.headline: Ukraine reports battlefield clashes, drone strikes despite ceasefire
World.detail: Ukrainian officials reported nearly 150 battlefield clashes and Russian drone strikes over the past 24 hours, despite a U.S.-brokered ceasefire between Kyiv and Moscow. One person was killed and three people were wounded in Russian strikes on Ukraine's southeastern Zaporizhzhia region.

World.headline: US President Donald Trump announces prisoner swap in 3-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire
World.detail: President Trump said that Russia and Ukraine agreed to a three-day ceasefire from May 9 to May 11 and the agreement will swap 1,000 prisoners from each country after Russia and Ukraine announced separate short-term ceasefire plans.

World.headline: Three hikers dead after Indonesia's Mount Dukono volcano erupts
World.detail: Three hikers died after Mount Dukono in Indonesia erupted, sending a thick ash column 10 kilometers above the summit. The hikers became stranded when Mount Dukono erupted.

World.headline: Cape Verde: Tiny nation, massive World Cup dream
World.detail: Cape Verde, with just over half a million people, has qualified for the World Cup for the first time. They are one of the tiniest countries ever to qualify for the tournament and are known as the Blue Sharks.

Zimbabwe.headline: Bar brawl ends in fatal stabbing
Zimbabwe.detail: A bar brawl resulted in a fatal stabbing incident. Details surrounding the incident are still emerging.

Zimbabwe.headline: 'It is too early to talk about the title, says Hardrock FC coach Kaindu as team goes atop log table
Zimbabwe.detail: Hardrock FC's coach Kaindu remains cautious about title aspirations despite the team's strong performance. Kaindu was also voted April Coach of The Month as Kawondera scoops top player's gong.

Zimbabwe.headline: Work Begins On New Interchange At Harare Drive–Airport Road Junction
Zimbabwe.detail: Construction has commenced on a new interchange at the Harare Drive-Airport Road junction. This project aims to improve traffic flow and reduce congestion in the area.

Zimbabwe.headline: TelOne appoint Saul Chaminuka as new head coach to save their season
Zimbabwe.detail: TelOne Football Club has appointed Saul Chaminuka as their new head coach in an effort to improve their performance. He faces an immediate test as they face high-flying Hardrock in Midlands derby.

Zimbabwe.headline: Prosecutor General Matanda-Moyo says prosecutions must be 'apolitical'
Zimbabwe.detail: Prosecutor General Matanda-Moyo has stated that prosecutions must be conducted in an apolitical manner. She emphasized the importance of impartiality in the justice system.
